body: SLS22 S2 checkpoint: Execution-Regime Monitor (ERM) built and compiled clean (0 errors, back to just the 1 pre-existing baseline warning). Rolling EWMA spread (general + separate 21:00-23:00 UTC rollover baseline) and slippage tracking sampled once per entry-decision evaluation and at every SafeOrderSend fill; rejection-rate window over last 20 attempts. Three-state hysteresis (NORMAL/DEGRADED/HOSTILE, UNKNOWN until 200-sample warmup) with asymmetric confirmation -- 5 consecutive samples to upgrade severity, 15 to downgrade, so it flags risk fast and relaxes slow. HOSTILE blocks new entries via CanTradeNow (single integration point alongside SEG); DEGRADED halves position size in OptimizePrecisionLotSize and skips signals whose edge margin (reused g_LastSignalMargin, no new metric invented) falls below the rolling median. Realized cost (spread+slippage per fill) now feeds CalculateDefensibleDailyGrowthRate as an R-unit haircut, so the compounding projection prices in real friction instead of assuming none. SEG/ERM interlock wired: if the fixed post-event window closes while ERM is still DEGRADED/HOSTILE, entries stay blocked for a bounded extension (3x the post-window) rather than reopening on the clock alone; falls back to the fixed window alone if ERM is UNKNOWN (absent/stale), per directive. Persistence: new guarded tail marker 0xE210 in SaveSystemState/LoadSystemState only -- SealArmedSave stays tail-free per precedent. Daily one-line summary wired into the existing daily-reset cadence. Moving to S3 (version bump) then S4 (fresh calibration + ghost validation).
